prompt:
Proceed and create everything, I set you up with copilot-github opus 4.6 which will take 3 premium tokens whatever all you do (whatever the amount of tokens you take next in one shot will take just 3 premium tokens, even if you output 5 million tokens !!! so try to make it worth our 3 premium tokens and do everything that has a greater impact then all the rest made a lot of sense.

As a practice during the work that you do, I would have you consider this as a ceremony and add and store data to the medecine-wheel - I have put in the `/workspace/repos/jgwill/medicine-wheel/.env` our online-lab so we shall be capable to visualize and interact with all the elements of your session, can't wait to visualize your ontologies and other parts.

My comprehension is that at the end, we will have :
* skill (medicine-wheel-mcp-usage or similar) , maybe more than one skill ??
* thin bash wrapper (mw) so you can do mw ceremony-open "Research session" from terminal  etc, could be the real thing with all the commands etc...
llms-medicine-wheel-mcp-tools.md in the llms/ which the path of the clone repo is `/workspace/repos/jgwill/llms-txt` and make sure it links well with what is also in `/workspace/repos/jgwill/llms-txt/llms.txt`,`/workspace/repos/jgwill/llms-txt/llms-full.txt` and `/workspace/repos/jgwill/llms-txt/docs` and you also have locally the `/workspace/repos/jgwill/medicine-wheel/llms-full.txt`,`/workspace/repos/jgwill/medicine-wheel/llms.txt` that you can establish a link between all of them using the CNAME in each of these path.  Keep this simple.  I saw `/workspace/repos/jgwill/llms-txt/llms-medicine-wheel-packages.txt` which might be outdated....

As you progress, if you see any bugs in any of the packages , you can revise them and do whatever you need to make it working.

primary.target: medicine-wheel MCP skills, thin CLI wrapper, documentation, and integrations linking local llms corpora to the medicine-wheel workspace

secondary.target: skill package (medicine-wheel-mcp-usage or similar) in the medicine-wheel repo
thin bash wrapper 'mw' providing ceremony CLI (e.g., mw ceremony-open "Research session")
secondary.dependency: skill package exists (can be developed in parallel but wrapper should reference skill commands)

secondary.target: llms-medicine-wheel-mcp-tools.md in medicine-wheel/llms/ documenting links to llms-txt corpora and local llms files
secondary.dependency: presence of llms files in /workspace/repos/jgwill/llms-txt and /workspace/repos/jgwill/medicine-wheel

secondary.target: establish simple links between /workspace/repos/jgwill/llms-txt/* and medicine-wheel llms files using CNAME or lightweight pointers (keep simple)
secondary.dependency: CNAME files exist and pointable; llms files available

secondary.target: ceremony session data into medicine-wheel store (use .env online-lab config to enable visualization)
secondary.dependency: .env contains valid 'online-lab' configuration and medicine-wheel store APIs are reachable

secondary.target: detect and fix bugs in packages encountered while integrating/creating the skill and wrapper
secondary.dependency: run tests / build after changes to detect issues

secondary.target: automated tests or basic validation steps to confirm skill and wrapper functionality
git commits and a PR (or branch) with changes; update README/docs to reference new commands and llms links
ensure online-lab visualization can display created ontologies and ceremony elements
secondary.dependency: stored ceremony data and online-lab connectivity

files_needed: CNAME files referenced in each path
package.json and repo scripts for medicine-wheel
assumptions: CNAME files referenced by user exist and are intended to signal linkage rather than DNS changes
write and git push permissions are available for local repository
user consents to automated bug fixes and commits
skill can be implemented in the existing repo language (likely JS/TS) without new toolchain

artifacts: small example ceremony session data JSON saved to medicine-wheel store
updates: llms links updated in /workspace/repos/jgwill/medicine-wheel/llms.txt or llms-full.txt (or add pointers)
CNAME entries documented/pointed-to in docs rather than modifying DNS
README.md updated with 'mw' usage and links to llms docs
package.json scripts updated if necessary to run wrapper or tests
communications: Create a git branch and open a PR with the new skill, wrapper, and docs
Create issues for any non-trivial bugs found and record fixes in PR
Add a short changelog entry and usage instructions in docs

east.text: Clarify scope of 'everything' — user expects skill(s), CLI wrapper, docs, and llms linking; confirm single vs multiple skills
Confirm .env 'online-lab' details and whether writing to the live online-lab is permitted

south.text: Inventory existing llms files and CNAMEs under /workspace/repos/jgwill/llms-txt and medicine-wheel; detect outdated llms-medicine-wheel-packages.txt
Research medicine-wheel store API/schema to store ceremony data in the correct format

west.text: Run repo build/tests after implementing skill and wrapper; validate CLI commands behave as documented
Peer-review or CI checks via PR; create issues for unresolved problems

north.text: Implement skill package scaffold and minimal functionality to interact with medicine-wheel store
Implement 'mw' bash wrapper exposing ceremony-open/close and linking to skill commands
Create llms-medicine-wheel-mcp-tools.md documenting links and pointers to corpora and usage examples

actionStack.text: Verify /workspace/repos/jgwill/medicine-wheel/.env and confirm 'online-lab' connectivity and credentials
Inventory llms files in /workspace/repos/jgwill/llms-txt and medicine-wheel and read CNAME files to understand desired linking
Create llms/llms-medicine-wheel-mcp-tools.md documenting corpora locations and simple link strategy using CNAME/pointers
Scaffold skill package (medicine-wheel-mcp-usage) with minimal commands to open/close ceremony and store session metadata
Implement 'mw' bash wrapper that invokes skill commands (ceremony-open, ceremony-close, status)
Store a sample ceremony session into the medicine-wheel store using .env online-lab settings so visualization can pick it up
actionStack.dependency: mw wrapper and skill functional; .env validated

actionStack.text: Run repository tests/build; detect and fix any package bugs encountered during integration
Commit changes on a feature branch, push, and open a PR with changelog and usage docs; create issues for remaining gaps
Confirm online-lab visualization renders created ontologies/ceremony elements and iterate if needed
actionStack.dependency: session data stored and PR merged or preview available

ambiguities.text: Scope of 'everything' (how many skills, depth of implementation, which packages may be changed)
ambiguities.suggestion: Ask user whether to implement one minimal skill or multiple feature-complete skills and whether automatic pushing/fixing is allowed

ambiguities.text: Details of .env 'online-lab' (credentials, endpoints, sandbox vs production)
ambiguities.suggestion: Request confirmation of environment details and permission to write session data to that endpoint

ambiguities.text: What 'link' via CNAME means practically (DNS change vs documentation pointer vs HTTP alias)
ambiguities.suggestion: Clarify whether to create documentation pointers/symlinks or to modify DNS records (the latter should not be done without explicit permission)

ambiguities.text: Acceptance criteria for 'make it working' when fixing package bugs (fix all vs triage vs patch only obvious issues)
ambiguities.suggestion: Ask whether to patch only build-breaking bugs or to open issues for deeper refactors
